steps.title: Identify what the question asks
steps.content:
The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two portraits. Scan the choices for whichever one highlights how the portraits differ from each other.
  • "The portraits, or artistic representations, of Carlos Mendoza and Arturo Reyes were both completed by Salazar in the early 2000s.": “Both completed… in the early 2000s” stresses a similarity, not a difference. ✗
  • "Salazar has completed portraits of numerous Mexican Americans, including sculptor Carlos Mendoza and writer Arturo Reyes.": This makes a general claim about Salazar’s body of work; it doesn’t contrast the two portraits at all. ✗
  • "While both are by Salazar, the 2003 portrait of Carlos Mendoza is a painting, and the 2016 portrait of Arturo Reyes is a drawing.": “While both are by Salazar” concedes the similarity, then the sentence pivots to the key difference: one is a painting, the other a drawing. ✓
  • "Carlos Mendoza was a Mexican American sculptor, and Arturo Reyes was a Mexican American writer.": This contrasts the people depicted, not the portraits themselves, so it misses the target. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
“While both are by Salazar, the 2003 portrait of Carlos Mendoza is a painting, and the 2016 portrait of Arturo Reyes is a drawing.” directly contrasts the two portraits by their medium — painting versus drawing — which is exactly the difference the notes support.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
